description: To 'shalori' is to speak or act on behalf of another person or group within a stated matter. The relation may come from election, appointment, or another accepted source, and the represented people can still correct or reject what is said for them. 'shaweri' (advocate) can support a position without claiming this standing.
articulatory_notes: Raise the tongue blade behind the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 'sh' and release into central 'a'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l', let air pass along its sides, and hold stressed true-mid rounded 'o'. Finish with a tap or short trill followed by high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: Broad 'sha' narrows into the rounded lateral centre of stressed 'lo', then final 'ri' turns the tongue once more and brightens the ending. The two tongue contacts give the word an audible path from one speaker towards another party.
usage_notes: In a transitive clause, the represented party is the direct object and comes before 'shalori'. Nearby clauses can name the matter and source. They can also set a duration, carry instructions, or establish a reporting relation. Within a clause, each relation still precedes what it modifies. Representation is the event-noun reading. A symbol is 'relo'; resemblance and description use their own words.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Representation lets a dispersed community coordinate beyond one meeting while keeping the representative answerable to the people whose position they carry.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The verb makes speaking for someone audible as a separate power relation. Their correction and refusal remain intact.

semantic_domains.communication: speaking on behalf of another party within a stated matter
semantic_domains.community: acting for represented people under a recognised source of standing
